rulings.Case Title: BETTY O'LEARY VS. ASSOCIATED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Dillingham Construction N.A., Inc.S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Sever Dillingham From The Trial Date
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 2, 2016, line 1. Defendant Dillingham Construction N.A., Inc.'s motion to sever Dillingham from the trial date: hearing required. The hearing will be at 9:30 a.m. Both sides must bring proposed orders to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. =(503/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM WARD ET AL VS. A.H. VOSS COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-16-276496 WITH CASE CGC-15-276396
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 2, 2016, line 2. PLAINTIFFS WILLIAM WARD, EMMA WARD'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-16-276496 WITH CASE CGC-15-276396. Plaintiffs' motion to consolidate cases CGC-15-276396 and CGC-16-276496 is granted. The two cases are consolidated for all purposes including trial. Henceforth, case no. 276396 shall be the lead case, any subsequent documents must be filed only in the lead case, and all documents filed in the consolidated case must include the caption and case number of the lead case, followed by the case number of the other consolidated case, in compliance with CRC 3.350. The consolidated case is added to the February 16, 2017 Trial Setting Conference. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. =(503/GLW)
